title: Counterpoint detail
summary: Counterpoints are architecturally important for the hiring-evaluator persona (signals intellectual honesty) but underused in v1 (4 of 276 items). v2 elevates discoverability and clarifies reader-facing label per Rule 2.

strategy.decision: Preserve v1's counterpoint reading shape (Executive Summary + numbered formal sections + challengesPost binding). Increase discoverability: surface counterpoints in the parent post's footer affordance, in the home-page Recent stream, and in the Library under a clear reader-facing label (D8).
strategy.why: Hiring-evaluator persona explicitly samples counterpoints to test for intellectual honesty (per persona file). v1's auto-link banner on the parent post (per blog template) is good but discoverability stops there. The 4-of-276 ratio suggests counterpoints aren't visible enough — v2 elevates them.
strategy.shipped: v1 counterpoint: Executive Summary + numbered formal sections + auto-link banner on parent post via challengesPost binding lookup. Counterpoint-detail surfaces in the counterpoints index (working well per F6-positive).
strategy.gap: v2 keeps the structure; changes are: (a) reader-facing label (D8) instead of the methodology term 'counterpoint', (b) elevated discoverability (parent-post footer + home-page Recent + Library), (c) format badge consistent with post-detail.
strategy.question: D8: what does v2 call this surface for readers? 'Counterpoint' is the methodology term and the schema field; 'the case against', 'stress-test', or another term may read better. Doesn't block build but blocks copy pass.

inheritsFromV1: auto-link banner on parent post via [slug].astro template

currentState.summary: v1 counterpoint: formal sections, Executive Summary, auto-link to parent post. 4 items currently.
currentState.annotation: v2 keeps the format and the auto-link, changes the reader-facing label, and elevates discoverability so the 4-of-276 ratio stops hiding intellectual-honesty signal.
